# Config Editing & Provider/Model Workflow

Stable mechanics for changing Hermes's configuration safely, plus the ordered workflow for switching providers/models so the change actually takes effect. Specific keys, sections, and provider names are **not** listed here — they go stale. Discover them live (see pointers below).

> Before running any command, confirm its flags with `hermes <cmd> --help` (SKILL.md Rule 2). If something here doesn't cover your case, fetch the docs index (Rule 1).

## Safe config-editing norms (adopted from Hermes's bundled autonomous-ai-agents skill)

Source: `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` and
`https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/skills/bundled/autonomous-ai-agents/autonomous-ai-agents-hermes-agent`

- **Use the dedicated commands, not blind YAML editing.** `hermes config edit` opens the file in `$EDITOR`; `hermes config set KEY VAL` sets a single value (dotted keys, e.g. `section.key`). These keep the file valid and avoid clobbering structure.
- **Settings vs secrets are separate stores.** Non-secret settings live in `config.yaml`; API keys and tokens live in `.env`. Find the paths with `hermes config path` and `hermes config env-path`.
- **API keys go through `hermes auth`** (the credential manager / pool), not hand-edited `.env`. Don't print keys in plaintext and never commit them to any repo.
- **Validate after editing:** `hermes config check` (missing/outdated config) and `hermes config migrate` (pull in new options / migrate deprecated settings). For models that have been retired, the top-level `hermes migrate` handles config migration too — check `hermes migrate --help` vs `hermes config migrate --help` for which applies.
- **`security.redact_secrets` is on by default** and snapshotted at startup. Toggling it mid-session (even via an env var from a tool call) does NOT affect the running process — change it from a terminal, then start a new session. This is intentional, so the agent can't turn off redaction on itself.
- **`approvals.mode`:** `manual` (default; prompts before destructive shell commands) / `smart` (auxiliary LLM auto-approves low-risk) / `off` (skips all prompts, same as `--yolo`). Per-invocation bypass: `hermes --yolo …`. YOLO/`off` does NOT disable secret redaction.
- **Restart to apply.** Config changes need a fresh process: exit and relaunch the CLI, or `/restart` the gateway. Tool/skill changes apply on `/reset` (new session), not mid-conversation (mid-conversation changes would break prompt caching).

> **The list of config sections, keys, defaults, and current values is not copied here** — it's the most stale-prone thing. To see what exists and what's set: read `~/.hermes/config.yaml` directly, or run `hermes config show`. For the full annotated reference: `https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/configuration`.

## Provider / model workflow (ordered — this is the stable part)

The point of ordering: a model/provider switch only "works" if the credential exists first and the process is restarted after. Do these in order.

1. **Configure the provider's credential first.** `hermes auth` (interactive) or `hermes auth add <provider>`. If you select a provider in the model picker before its credential is set up, it'll only fail at runtime. Verify with `hermes auth list` / `hermes auth status`.
2. **Select the default model / provider.** `hermes model` (interactive picker; `--refresh` re-fetches each provider's live model list). The persistent provider lives in `config.yaml` under `model.provider`.
3. **Configure fallback** (tried when the primary model fails — rate-limit, overload, connection errors). `hermes fallback --help` for `add` / `list` / `remove` / `clear`.
4. **Restart so it takes effect.** Exit and relaunch the CLI, or `/restart` the gateway.
5. **Verify what's actually live.** `hermes status` (add `--all` for a redacted full view) and `hermes model` confirm which provider/model is in effect — don't assume the edit took.

> **The provider list and their env-var / auth methods are not copied here** (they change often). To see what's available and how each authenticates: run `hermes model`, or read `https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/integrations/providers`. For a one-off override without changing config: `hermes -m <model> --provider <provider> …` (see `hermes chat --help`).

# Config Editing & Provider/Model Workflow

Stable mechanics for changing Hermes's configuration safely, plus the ordered workflow for switching providers/models so the change actually takes effect. Specific keys, sections, and provider names are **not** listed here — they go stale. Discover them live (see pointers below).

> Before running any command, confirm its flags with `hermes <cmd> --help` (SKILL.md Rule 2). If something here doesn't cover your case, fetch the docs index (Rule 1).

## Safe config-editing norms (adopted from Hermes's bundled autonomous-ai-agents skill)

Source (local): `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` and the docs page `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/user-guide/skills/bundled/autonomous-ai-agents/` (web mirror: `https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/skills/bundled/autonomous-ai-agents/`).

- **Use the dedicated commands, not blind YAML editing.** `hermes config edit` opens the file in `$EDITOR`; `hermes config set KEY VAL` sets a single value (dotted keys, e.g. `section.key`). These keep the file valid and avoid clobbering structure.
- **Settings vs secrets are separate stores.** Non-secret settings live in `config.yaml`; API keys and tokens live in `.env`. Find the paths with `hermes config path` and `hermes config env-path`.
- **API keys go through `hermes auth`** (the credential manager / pool), not hand-edited `.env`. Don't print keys in plaintext and never commit them to any repo.

# Troubleshooting & Recovery

Stable diagnostic and rollback **processes**. Symptom lists go stale and are not copied here — when a symptom isn't covered, fetch the docs index (SKILL.md Rule 1). Confirm flags with `hermes <cmd> --help` before running (Rule 2).

## Diagnostic triad (start here, in order)

1. `hermes doctor` — checks config and dependencies. `hermes doctor --fix` attempts automatic fixes. It also surfaces security advisories (ack with `hermes doctor --ack <id>`).
2. `hermes status` — component status; `hermes status --all` for a redacted full view, `--deep` for slower thorough checks.
3. Logs — `hermes logs` (defaults to `agent.log`, last 50 lines). Useful flags: `-f` (follow), `--level ERROR`, `--component gateway|agent|cron`, `--since 1h`, `hermes logs list` to see available files. Files live under `~/.hermes/logs/`.

## Common failure shapes (process, not symptom catalog)

- **Model / provider not working:** `hermes doctor` first → re-authenticate the provider with `hermes auth` (or `hermes auth add <provider>`) → confirm the key/credential exists. See `reference/config-editing.md` for the full provider workflow.
- **A change didn't take effect:** this is almost always a restart issue, not a broken edit.
  - Tool / skill changes → `/reset` (new session); they don't apply mid-conversation.
  - Config changes → `/restart` the gateway, or exit and relaunch the CLI.
  - Source-code changes → restart the process entirely.
- **Update broke the install / rollback:** Hermes keeps backups in `~/.hermes/backups/`; restore one with `hermes import <zipfile>` (`--force` to overwrite). A `~/.hermes/hermes-agent.broken-<timestamp>` directory is the old source set aside by a failed/risky `hermes update` — it's a leftover, not active. You can also create your own safety net before risky operations with `hermes backup` (`--quick` for just critical state). Filesystem checkpoints (the `/rollback` history) are managed via `hermes checkpoints` (`status` / `prune` / `clear`).
- **Retired / deprecated model or settings after an update:** `hermes config migrate` (and/or top-level `hermes migrate` for retired models — check each `--help`).
- **Anything else / deeper:** fetch the docs index (Rule 1) and route to the specific guide. The local bundled skill at `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` has a longer (faster-staling) troubleshooting section worth skimming for gateway/platform-specific cases.

## Recovery sequencing (when an update or edit left it broken)

1. **Diagnose before changing anything** — `hermes doctor` and `hermes status` to see what's actually wrong; check `hermes logs --level ERROR`.
2. **Snapshot current state** — `hermes backup --quick` before you start undoing things, so a bad recovery is itself reversible.
3. **Restore the most recent good backup** — `hermes import <zipfile>` from `~/.hermes/backups/`.
4. **Re-validate** — `hermes config check`, then `hermes doctor` again; relaunch and confirm with `hermes status`.

A `~/.hermes/hermes-agent.broken-<timestamp>` directory is the old source set aside by a failed/risky `hermes update` — it's a leftover, not active. You can also create your own safety net before risky operations with `hermes backup` (`--quick` for just critical state). Filesystem checkpoints (the `/rollback` history) are managed via `hermes checkpoints` (`status` / `prune` / `clear`).
- **Retired / deprecated model or settings after an update:** `hermes config migrate` (and/or top-level `hermes migrate` for retired models — check each `--help`).

-- **Anything else / deeper:** fetch the docs index (Rule 1) and route to the specific guide. The local bundled skill at `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` has a longer (faster-staling) troubleshooting section worth skimming for gateway/platform-specific cases.

## 改动总结

Explore subagent 确认本机有完整离线文档:`~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/`(339 个 `.md`,5.5M,git 安装路径稳定;`hermes update` 替换源码但保留该相对路径)。

据此修改:

**SKILL.md**

- **本机事实**新增一条:完整文档库在本地,优先于 web。

**reference/config-editing.md**

**reference/troubleshooting.md**

**保留的唯一 web 链接**:llms.txt 索引(本机确认**不存在**本地等价物),作为非 git 安装或本地找不到时的兜底——符合「本地优先」且不丢失退路。

设计原则不变:仍是「路由+发现,不存副本」,只是把「发现」的指针从 web 换成了更快的本地文档库。需要我提交时再说。

input.prompt:
Review a newly-created agent skill at `~/Coder/skills/skills/workflows/hermes-agent/`. It has 4 files: `SKILL.md`, `reference/config-editing.md`, `reference/troubleshooting.md`, `reference/operations.md`. Read all 4 first.

Context — the skill's design philosophy (judge against this, don't violate it):
- "Route + discover, no copies": stale-prone lists (subcommands, providers, config sections, slash commands, symptoms) must NOT be copied into the skill. They should be routed to live sources: the file `~/.hermes/config.yaml`, `hermes <cmd> --help`, `hermes config|tools list|skills list`, the local docs at `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/`, or web as fallback.
- The skill keeps ONLY stable content: two hard rules, machine facts, workflows, safety norms, gotchas.
- All skill files are written in English (repo convention).
- This is an OPERATIONS skill for an ALREADY-INSTALLED Hermes (Nous Research agent framework). GUI `/Applications/Hermes.app` and CLI `~/.local/bin/hermes` share `~/.hermes/` home.

Your TWO tasks:

**TASK 1 — Verify every path/command reference is correct (use Bash, read-only).**
For each filesystem path mentioned in the 4 files, confirm it actually exists on this machine. Notably:
- `~/.hermes/` and its children referenced: `config.yaml`, `.env`, `auth.json`, `logs/`, `sessions/`, `state.db`, `skills/`, `backups/`, `hermes-agent/`
- `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/` and the SPECIFIC doc files cited: `user-guide/configuration.md`, `integrations/providers.md`, `reference/faq.md`, and the bundled skill page dir `user-guide/skills/bundled/autonomous-ai-agents/`
- `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md`
- `~/.local/bin/hermes`, `/Applications/Hermes.app`, `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/hermes`
- The claim "339 .md files" under the docs root — spot check the count is roughly right.
For each `hermes <subcommand>` referenced in workflows (e.g. `hermes config show`, `hermes auth add`, `hermes model`, `hermes fallback`, `hermes doctor`, `hermes status`, `hermes logs`, `hermes import`, `hermes backup`, `hermes checkpoints`, `hermes config check/migrate`, `hermes migrate`, `hermes sessions`, `hermes cron`, `hermes send`, `hermes desktop`, `hermes chat -q`, `hermes -z`), verify the subcommand actually exists by checking `hermes <cmd> --help` (run it; it's safe, read-only). Flag any command or flag that does NOT exist or is named differently. Do NOT run any state-changing command (no `set`, no `edit`, no `import`, no `update`, no `auth add` for real — just `--help` on them).
Report a table: reference → exists? (yes/no) → note.

**TASK 2 — Redundancy & concision review.**
Identify: (a) content repeated across files or within a file that could be stated once and cross-referenced; (b) filler / low-value sentences that don't earn their place; (c) anything that violates "route + discover, no copies" (i.e. a copied list that will go stale); (d) places that are too terse and would actually benefit from one more sentence. Propose SPECIFIC, concrete edits (quote the text, suggest the replacement or deletion). Be concise and prioritize — list the top items by impact, don't nitpick every word.

Do NOT modify any files. Report findings only.

content.text:
Confirmed: `model.provider` is the config path (verified), and `-z/--oneshot` help explicitly says "approvals are auto-bypassed" — matching the skill. All verifications complete. I have everything needed for both tasks.

---

# TASK 1 — Path & Command Verification

Everything was checked read-only on this machine. **Two real problems, one minor framing issue.**

## Filesystem paths

| Reference | Exists? | Note |
|---|---|---|
| `~/.hermes/` | yes | |
| `~/.hermes/config.yaml` | yes | |
| `~/.hermes/.env` | yes | |
| `~/.hermes/auth.json` | yes | |
| `~/.hermes/logs/` | yes | |
| `~/.hermes/sessions/` | yes | |
| `~/.hermes/state.db` | yes | |
| `~/.hermes/skills/` | yes | |
| **`~/.hermes/backups/`** | **NO** | **Does not exist. `hermes backup` default output is `~/hermes-backup-<timestamp>.zip` (home dir, not `~/.hermes/backups/`). See Finding A.** |
| `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/` | yes | |
| `~/.local/bin/hermes` | yes | confirmed thin bash wrapper: `exec "/Users/jfmoe/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/hermes" "$@"` — matches skill exactly |
| `/Applications/Hermes.app` | yes | |
| `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/hermes` | yes | |
| `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/` | yes | **339 `.md` files exactly** — the cited count is correct |
| `.../docs/user-guide/configuration.md` | yes | |
| `.../docs/integrations/providers.md` | yes | |
| `.../docs/reference/faq.md` | yes | |
| `.../docs/user-guide/skills/bundled/autonomous-ai-agents/` | yes | dir exists; contains 5 `.md` pages (no index.md, but cited as a dir — fine) |
| `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` | yes | |
| `~/.hermes/checkpoints/` (implied by `hermes checkpoints`) | yes | confirmed by `checkpoints --help` text |
| `~/.hermes/hermes-agent.broken-<timestamp>` | yes | a real one is present: `hermes-agent.broken-20260608-140436` — the skill's claim is accurate |

## Commands / subcommands / flags

| Reference | Exists? | Note |
|---|---|---|
| `hermes config show / edit / set / path / env-path / check / migrate` | yes | all 7 present |
| `hermes auth` / `auth add <provider>` / `auth list` / `auth status` | yes | `auth add` takes positional `provider`; also has remove/reset/logout/spotify |
| `hermes model` (`--refresh`) | yes | `--refresh` confirmed |
| `hermes fallback` (`list`/`add`/`remove`/`clear`) | yes | all present (plus `ls`/`rm` aliases) |
| `hermes doctor` (`--fix`, `--ack <id>`) | yes | all confirmed |
| `hermes status` (`--all`, `--deep`) | yes | both confirmed |
| `hermes logs` (`-f`, `--level`, `--component`, `--since`, `list`) | yes | all confirmed; default agent.log/50 lines correct. `--component` values include `gateway|agent|cron` (skill's example) plus tools/cli/gui |
| `hermes import <zipfile>` (`--force`) | yes | confirmed |
| `hermes backup` (`--quick`) | yes | `--quick` = `-q`; also `-l LABEL`. **But default output path is wrong in skill — see Finding A** |
| `hermes checkpoints` (`status`/`prune`/`clear`) | yes | all present (+ `list` alias, `clear-legacy`); help confirms `~/.hermes/checkpoints/` |
| `hermes config check` / `hermes config migrate` | yes | both confirmed |
| **`hermes migrate`** | yes (narrower) | **Exists but only has subcommand `{xai}` — it is a model-specific migrator (`migrate xai`), NOT a general "top-level migrate for retired models" with a broad help. See Finding B.** |
| `hermes sessions` (list/browse/rename/export/prune/delete/stats) | yes | all present (+ `optimize`) |
| `hermes cron` (create/list/edit/pause/resume/run/remove) | yes | all present; `create` aliased `add` |
| `hermes send` (`--list`, target syntax) | yes | confirmed; reuses `~/.hermes/.env + config.yaml` exactly as skill says |
| `hermes desktop` (alias `gui`) | yes | confirmed builds+launches Electron |
| `hermes chat -q "…"` | yes | `-q/--query` is correct (note: quiet is `-Q/--quiet`, distinct — skill gets this right) |
| `hermes chat --tui` / `--cli` / `--continue` / `--resume` | yes | all confirmed as flags |
| `hermes -z "…"` (`--oneshot`) | yes | confirmed; help literally says "approvals are auto-bypassed" — matches skill's safety note |
| `hermes -m <model> --provider <provider>` | yes | both are top-level + chat flags |
| `hermes tools list` / `hermes skills list` | yes | both work |
| config path `model.provider` | yes | confirmed in config.yaml (`model:` → `provider:`) |

### Finding A (real bug) — `~/.hermes/backups/` is wrong
Three places assert backups live in `~/.hermes/backups/`:
- SKILL.md line 42: "backups land in `~/.hermes/backups/`"
- troubleshooting.md line 18: "Hermes keeps backups in `~/.hermes/backups/`"
- troubleshooting.md line 26: "Restore the most recent good backup — `hermes import <zipfile>` from `~/.hermes/backups/`"

`backup --help` shows the default output is `~/hermes-backup-<timestamp>.zip` (home directory), and `~/.hermes/backups/` does not exist on this machine. `hermes update`'s automatic backups may go elsewhere, but the skill states this as a flat fact and points the user to a nonexistent directory. **Fix:** either say "backups are written to your home dir by default (`~/hermes-backup-<timestamp>.zip`); locate them with `ls ~/hermes-backup-*.zip`" or, better per the skill's own philosophy, don't hardcode a path — route to `hermes backup --help` for the output location.

### Finding B (minor framing) — `hermes migrate` is narrower than described
config-editing.md line 14 and troubleshooting.md line 19 imply `hermes migrate` is a general top-level retired-model migrator parallel to `hermes config migrate`. Actually `hermes migrate` only exposes `migrate xai`. The "check each `--help` for which applies" hedge keeps it technically safe, but the phrasing oversells it. Minor.

---

# TASK 2 — Redundancy & Concision (top items by impact)

### 1. Safety norms are stated nearly verbatim in two files (highest-impact redundancy)
The `redact_secrets`, `approvals.mode`, "edit with dedicated commands", "API keys via `hermes auth`", and "restart to apply" bullets appear in **both** SKILL.md (lines 66–70) and config-editing.md (lines 11–17), with config-editing.md being the more complete version.

SKILL.md line 64 already says "Details and the exact edit commands are in `reference/config-editing.md`." So SKILL.md is duplicating the detail it claims to delegate. **Fix:** in SKILL.md, collapse the 5 bullets (66–70) to 2–3 one-liners (the two genuinely load-bearing ones: redact_secrets-is-snapshotted-at-startup, and approvals.mode-default-manual) plus the existing cross-reference. Let config-editing.md own the full list. Saves ~6 lines and removes the drift risk of maintaining the approvals/redact wording in two places.

### 2. "Restart to apply" / "a change didn't take effect" is repeated in three files
- config-editing.md line 17 ("Restart to apply…")
- SKILL.md line 70 ("Most config, tool, and skill changes need a restart…")
- troubleshooting.md lines 14–17 ("A change didn't take effect: …/reset for tools, /restart for config…")

This is the single most-repeated concept. The tool→`/reset`, config→`/restart`, source→full-restart mapping is stated 3×. **Fix:** keep the full mapping in exactly one place (troubleshooting.md's version is the clearest), and have the other two cross-reference it ("restart semantics: see troubleshooting.md"). Currently the wording differs slightly between copies, which is exactly the staleness the skill is trying to avoid.

### 3. The bundled-skill pointer is repeated in all four files
"`~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` … list-heavy / faster-staling" appears in SKILL.md (44), config-editing.md (9), troubleshooting.md (20), operations.md (33). Four mentions with four slightly different caveats. Two of them (troubleshooting + operations) earn their place because they point at *specific* content (longer troubleshooting section; tmux/spawn examples). The SKILL.md (44) and config-editing.md (9) mentions are generic. **Fix:** keep the two specific pointers; in SKILL.md reduce to one line in Machine facts and drop the duplicate caveat from config-editing.md (line 9 can just cite the docs page, not re-explain the bundled skill).

### 4. Filler that doesn't earn its place
- SKILL.md line 10 ("The design choice behind this skill: …") restates the routing philosophy that the file already demonstrates structurally and that line 8 plus the routing table convey. It's meta-narration aimed at a reader, not an operator. **Fix:** cut or shorten to one clause. The two hard rules + routing table already enforce the philosophy without explaining it.
- config-editing.md line 23 ("The point of ordering: a model/provider switch only 'works' if the credential exists first and the process is restarted after.") — this is genuinely useful (explains *why* the order matters) and should stay; not filler.

### 5. Possible "copied list" creep (philosophy check)
The skill is generally faithful to "route + discover," but a few inline lists risk going stale:
- operations.md line 23 lists "Common stable ones: `/reset`, `/restart`, `/model`, `/config`, `/rollback`, `/skill <name>`." This is a copied slash-command list — exactly what Rule says not to do — even though the line above correctly says to use `/help`. These six are reasonably stable, but it's the same category the skill routes around. **Judgment:** borderline-acceptable since labeled "stable," but if you want strict adherence, cut to one example and rely on `/help`.
- operations.md lines 29–31 ("Deeper areas") list ~12 subsystem names (delegate_task, curator, kanban, MCP, plugins, profiles, webhooks, gateway…). These are out-of-scope pointers, not operational lists, so staleness impact is low — but it's the largest copied enumeration in the skill. Confirmed all exist as real subcommands (`kanban`, `curator`, `plugins`, `profile`, `mcp`, `webhook`, `gateway` are all in `hermes --help`), so it's accurate today. Keep, but it's the place most likely to drift.

### 6. Too terse — would benefit from one more sentence
- troubleshooting.md line 18 mentions `hermes import <zipfile>` for rollback but (given Finding A) never tells the operator *where to find* a zipfile to import. Add one clause: "find available backups with `ls ~/hermes-backup-*.zip` (or wherever `hermes backup --help` reports)." This directly fixes the dead `~/.hermes/backups/` reference and the terseness in one edit.
- SKILL.md line 42 "`.install_method=git`" is stated as a fact but I couldn't find that literal key in `config.yaml` (the install is clearly git/source — venv + source + docs all present). Consider softening to "source/git install (venv + bundled source + local docs present)" rather than citing a specific config key that may not exist, OR verify the key's real location before asserting it.

### Net recommendation
The skill is well-built and faithful to its philosophy; commands and paths are ~95% accurate. Priorities: **(1) fix the `~/.hermes/backups/` path bug (Finding A) — it's a wrong path a user will follow**, (2) de-duplicate the restart-semantics and safety-norms content down to one authoritative location each with cross-references, (3) soften the `hermes migrate` framing (Finding B) and the `.install_method=git` assertion.

| `hermes chat -q "…"` | yes | `-q/--query` is correct (note: quiet is `-Q/--quiet`, distinct — skill gets this right) |
| `hermes chat --tui` / `--cli` / `--continue` / `--resume` | yes | all confirmed as flags |
| `hermes -z "…"` (`--oneshot`) | yes | confirmed; help literally says "approvals are auto-bypassed" — matches skill's safety note |
| `hermes -m <model> --provider <provider>` | yes | both are top-level + chat flags |
| `hermes tools list` / `hermes skills list` | yes | both work |
| config path `model.provider` | yes | confirmed in config.yaml (`model:` → `provider:`) |

### Finding A (real bug) — `~/.hermes/backups/` is wrong
Three places assert backups live in `~/.hermes/backups/`:
- SKILL.md line 42: "backups land in `~/.hermes/backups/`"
- troubleshooting.md line 18: "Hermes keeps backups in `~/.hermes/backups/`"
- troubleshooting.md line 26: "Restore the most recent good backup — `hermes import <zipfile>` from `~/.hermes/backups/`"

`backup --help` shows the default output is `~/hermes-backup-<timestamp>.zip` (home directory), and `~/.hermes/backups/` does not exist on this machine. `hermes update`'s automatic backups may go elsewhere, but the skill states this as a flat fact and points the user to a nonexistent directory. **Fix:** either say "backups are written to your home dir by default (`~/hermes-backup-<timestamp>.zip`); locate them with `ls ~/hermes-backup-*.zip`" or, better per the skill's own philosophy, don't hardcode a path — route to `hermes backup --help` for the output location.

### Finding B (minor framing) — `hermes migrate` is narrower than described
config-editing.md line 14 and troubleshooting.md line 19 imply `hermes migrate` is a general top-level retired-model migrator parallel to `hermes config migrate`. Actually `hermes migrate` only exposes `migrate xai`. The "check each `--help` for which applies" hedge keeps it technically safe, but the phrasing oversells it. Minor.

---

# TASK 2 — Redundancy & Concision (top items by impact)

### 1. Safety norms are stated nearly verbatim in two files (highest-impact redundancy)
The `redact_secrets`, `approvals.mode`, "edit with dedicated commands", "API keys via `hermes auth`", and "restart to apply" bullets appear in **both** SKILL.md (lines 66–70) and config-editing.md (lines 11–17), with config-editing.md being the more complete version.

SKILL.md line 64 already says "Details and the exact edit commands are in `reference/config-editing.md`." So SKILL.md is duplicating the detail it claims to delegate. **Fix:** in SKILL.md, collapse the 5 bullets (66–70) to 2–3 one-liners (the two genuinely load-bearing ones: redact_secrets-is-snapshotted-at-startup, and approvals.mode-default-manual) plus the existing cross-reference. Let config-editing.md own the full list. Saves ~6 lines and removes the drift risk of maintaining the approvals/redact wording in two places.

### 2. "Restart to apply" / "a change didn't take effect" is repeated in three files
- config-editing.md line 17 ("Restart to apply…")
- SKILL.md line 70 ("Most config, tool, and skill changes need a restart…")
- troubleshooting.md lines 14–17 ("A change didn't take effect: …/reset for tools, /restart for config…")

This is the single most-repeated concept. The tool→`/reset`, config→`/restart`, source→full-restart mapping is stated 3×. **Fix:** keep the full mapping in exactly one place (troubleshooting.md's version is the clearest), and have the other two cross-reference it ("restart semantics: see troubleshooting.md"). Currently the wording differs slightly between copies, which is exactly the staleness the skill is trying to avoid.

### 3. The bundled-skill pointer is repeated in all four files
"`~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` … list-heavy / faster-staling" appears in SKILL.md (44), config-editing.md (9), troubleshooting.md (20), operations.md (33). Four mentions with four slightly different caveats. Two of them (troubleshooting + operations) earn their place because they point at *specific* content (longer troubleshooting section; tmux/spawn examples). The SKILL.md (44) and config-editing.md (9) mentions are generic. **Fix:** keep the two specific pointers; in SKILL.md reduce to one line in Machine facts and drop the duplicate caveat from config-editing.md (line 9 can just cite the docs page, not re-explain the bundled skill).

### 4. Filler that doesn't earn its place
- SKILL.md line 10 ("The design choice behind this skill: …") restates the routing philosophy that the file already demonstrates structurally and that line 8 plus the routing table convey. It's meta-narration aimed at a reader, not an operator. **Fix:** cut or shorten to one clause. The two hard rules + routing table already enforce the philosophy without explaining it.
- config-editing.md line 23 ("The point of ordering: a model/provider switch only 'works' if the credential exists first and the process is restarted after.") — this is genuinely useful (explains *why* the order matters) and should stay; not filler.

### 5. Possible "copied list" creep (philosophy check)
The skill is generally faithful to "route + discover," but a few inline lists risk going stale:
- operations.md line 23 lists "Common stable ones: `/reset`, `/restart`, `/model`, `/config`, `/rollback`, `/skill <name>`." This is a copied slash-command list — exactly what Rule says not to do — even though the line above correctly says to use `/help`. These six are reasonably stable, but it's the same category the skill routes around. **Judgment:** borderline-acceptable since labeled "stable," but if you want strict adherence, cut to one example and rely on `/help`.
- operations.md lines 29–31 ("Deeper areas") list ~12 subsystem names (delegate_task, curator, kanban, MCP, plugins, profiles, webhooks, gateway…). These are out-of-scope pointers, not operational lists, so staleness impact is low — but it's the largest copied enumeration in the skill. Confirmed all exist as real subcommands (`kanban`, `curator`, `plugins`, `profile`, `mcp`, `webhook`, `gateway` are all in `hermes --help`), so it's accurate today. Keep, but it's the place most likely to drift.

### 6. Too terse — would benefit from one more sentence
- troubleshooting.md line 18 mentions `hermes import <zipfile>` for rollback but (given Finding A) never tells the operator *where to find* a zipfile to import. Add one clause: "find available backups with `ls ~/hermes-backup-*.zip` (or wherever `hermes backup --help` reports)." This directly fixes the dead `~/.hermes/backups/` reference and the terseness in one edit.
- SKILL.md line 42 "`.install_method=git`" is stated as a fact but I couldn't find that literal key in `config.yaml` (the install is clearly git/source — venv + source + docs all present). Consider softening to "source/git install (venv + bundled source + local docs present)" rather than citing a specific config key that may not exist, OR verify the key's real location before asserting it.

### Net recommendation
The skill is well-built and faithful to its philosophy; commands and paths are ~95% accurate. Priorities: **(1) fix the `~/.hermes/backups/` path bug (Finding A) — it's a wrong path a user will follow**, (2) de-duplicate the restart-semantics and safety-norms content down to one authoritative location each with cross-references, (3) soften the `hermes migrate` framing (Finding B) and the `.install_method=git` assertion.
Review 很有价值。两个事实问题我先自己核实再改——尤其 backup 路径,spec(引自 config.yaml `updates:` 段)说 `hermes update` 的备份在 `~/.hermes/backups/`,而 review 说 `hermes backup` 命令默认输出在 `~/`。这是两个不同的东西,我之前混淆了。核实一下。

---
name: hermes-agent
description: "Use when configuring, running, or maintaining a Hermes Agent (Nous Research's open-source agent framework) that is ALREADY installed on this machine — switching its model or provider, editing any config, running it day-to-day (chat/TUI/sessions/cron/send), or troubleshooting and repairing it (updates, rollback, doctor, logs, backup restore). Trigger even when the user just says 'Hermes is broken', 'change Hermes's model', 'how do I run Hermes in the background', or names `~/.hermes/`, `~/.local/bin/hermes`, or `/Applications/Hermes.app`. Does NOT cover first-time install/bootstrap, contributing to Hermes's source, or Windows-only issues."
---

# Hermes Agent — Operations

Hermes Agent (Nous Research) is already installed on this machine. This skill helps you **configure, run, and troubleshoot** it. It does not cover first-time install, source-code contribution, or Windows.

The design choice behind this skill: Hermes changes fast, so anything that goes stale — the list of subcommands, providers, config sections, slash commands, symptoms — is **not** copied here. Instead you discover the current truth from live sources at runtime. What lives here is only the stable stuff: hard rules, machine facts, workflows, safety norms, and gotchas.

## Two hard rules (these outrank answering from memory)

**Rule 1 — When unsure or uncovered, consult the docs. Do not guess.**
This machine has the **complete doc set locally** (git install) — prefer it over the web; it's the same content, offline and greppable. The docs root is:

```
~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/        # 339 .md files, full Docusaurus site
```

Grep/glob there to find the relevant guide (e.g. `grep -ril <topic> ~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/`), then read the file. Key sections: `user-guide/`, `integrations/`, `reference/`, `developer-guide/`. If the local docs are absent (non-git install) or you still can't find it, fall back to the web index and route from it to the specific guide URL:

```
https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/assets/files/llms-d4972c57170916efd83766ae50c3bb3d.txt
```

Never invent commands, flags, config keys, or provider names.

**Rule 2 — Verify exact command flags/arguments with `--help` before running.**
Hermes's subcommands and flags shift between versions. Before you run any specific command, check it live:

```bash
hermes <subcommand> --help        # e.g. hermes config --help, hermes auth --help
```

Do not reconstruct flags from memory. The `--help` output is the source of truth for what exists right now.

## Machine facts (stable structure — use for orientation)

- **Home:** `~/.hermes/` — config (`config.yaml`), secrets (`.env`), `auth.json`, `logs/`, `sessions/`, `state.db`, `skills/`, source at `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/`.
- **GUI and CLI share the same home.** `/Applications/Hermes.app` and the CLI `~/.local/bin/hermes` both run against `~/.hermes/`. The CLI is a thin bash wrapper that `exec`s `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/hermes`. **Any config/auth change affects both GUI and CLI.** There is no separate per-surface config.
- **Source install:** `.install_method=git`, source in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/`. Official updates go through `hermes update`; backups land in `~/.hermes/backups/`.
- **Full docs are local:** `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/` holds the complete Docusaurus doc site (339 `.md` files). Prefer it over the web. `hermes update` replaces the source dir but keeps this relative path stable.
- A local copy of Hermes's own bundled ops skill lives at `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` — useful as a fuller (but list-heavy, faster-staling) reference. Its lists are exactly what this skill routes around; treat its **stable** guidance (safety, gotchas) as aligned with the rules below.

## Routing — intent → reference file + discovery command

| You want to… | Read | Discover current truth with |
|---|---|---|
| Configure / switch provider or model, edit any config | `reference/config-editing.md` | `~/.hermes/config.yaml` or `hermes config show`; `hermes model`; `hermes config --help`, `hermes auth --help` |
| Run / use it day-to-day (chat, TUI, sessions, cron, send) | `reference/operations.md` | `hermes --help`; `hermes <cmd> --help`; `/help` in-session |
| Diagnose / maintain / update / roll back | `reference/troubleshooting.md` | `hermes doctor`, `hermes status`, `hermes logs` |

**Discovery commands (what exists right now, never copied into this skill):**

- Subcommands: `hermes --help`, then `hermes <cmd> --help` for flags.
- Config: read `~/.hermes/config.yaml` directly, or `hermes config show`; paths via `hermes config path` / `hermes config env-path`.
- Tools: `hermes tools list`. Skills: `hermes skills list`. Providers/models: `hermes model`.
- In-session slash commands: `/help`.
- Anything deeper or uncovered: **Rule 1** — grep the local docs at `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/` (full offline doc set), web index only as fallback.

## Safety norms (aligned with Hermes's bundled autonomous-ai-agents skill)

These are adopted directly from Hermes's own ops skill — don't invent a parallel set. Details and the exact edit commands are in `reference/config-editing.md`.

- **Edit config with the dedicated commands, not blind YAML edits:** `hermes config edit` / `hermes config set KEY VAL`. Settings live in `config.yaml`; secrets live in `.env`.
- **API keys go through `hermes auth`** (the credential manager), not hand-edited `.env`. Never print secrets in plaintext, never commit them to any repo.
- **`security.redact_secrets` is on by default** and snapshotted at process startup — toggling it mid-session does NOT take effect; it needs a restart. This is deliberate, so an LLM can't disable redaction on itself mid-task.
- **Command approval (`approvals.mode`):** `manual` (default — prompts before destructive commands) / `smart` / `off` (equivalent to `--yolo`). YOLO / `off` does NOT disable secret redaction; they're independent.
- **Most config, tool, and skill changes need a restart to take effect:** exit and relaunch the CLI; `/restart` the gateway; `/reset` (new session) for tool/skill changes. Confirm before running anything destructive in `manual`-mode environments.

---
name: hermes-agent
description: "Use when configuring, running, or maintaining a Hermes Agent (Nous Research's open-source agent framework) that is ALREADY installed on this machine — switching its model or provider, editing any config, running it day-to-day (chat/TUI/sessions/cron/send), or troubleshooting and repairing it (updates, rollback, doctor, logs, backup restore). Trigger even when the user just says 'Hermes is broken', 'change Hermes's model', 'how do I run Hermes in the background', or names `~/.hermes/`, `~/.local/bin/hermes`, or `/Applications/Hermes.app`. Does NOT cover first-time install/bootstrap, contributing to Hermes's source, or Windows-only issues."
---

# Hermes Agent — Operations

Hermes Agent (Nous Research) is already installed on this machine. This skill helps you **configure, run, and troubleshoot** it. It does not cover first-time install, source-code contribution, or Windows.

The design choice behind this skill: Hermes changes fast, so anything that goes stale — the list of subcommands, providers, config sections, slash commands, symptoms — is **not** copied here. Instead you discover the current truth from live sources at runtime. What lives here is only the stable stuff: hard rules, machine facts, workflows, safety norms, and gotchas.

## Two hard rules (these outrank answering from memory)

**Rule 1 — When unsure or uncovered, consult the docs. Do not guess.**
This machine has the **complete doc set locally** (git install) — prefer it over the web; it's the same content, offline and greppable. The docs root is:

```
~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/        # 339 .md files, full Docusaurus site
```

Grep/glob there to find the relevant guide (e.g. `grep -ril <topic> ~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/`), then read the file. Key sections: `user-guide/`, `integrations/`, `reference/`, `developer-guide/`. If the local docs are absent (non-git install) or you still can't find it, fall back to the web index and route from it to the specific guide URL:

```
https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/assets/files/llms-d4972c57170916efd83766ae50c3bb3d.txt
```

Never invent commands, flags, config keys, or provider names.

**Rule 2 — Verify exact command flags/arguments with `--help` before running.**
Hermes's subcommands and flags shift between versions. Before you run any specific command, check it live:

```bash
hermes <subcommand> --help        # e.g. hermes config --help, hermes auth --help
```

Do not reconstruct flags from memory. The `--help` output is the source of truth for what exists right now.

## Machine facts (stable structure — use for orientation)

- **Home:** `~/.hermes/` — config (`config.yaml`), secrets (`.env`), `auth.json`, `logs/`, `sessions/`, `state.db`, `skills/`, source at `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/`.
- **GUI and CLI share the same home.** `/Applications/Hermes.app` and the CLI `~/.local/bin/hermes` both run against `~/.hermes/`. The CLI is a thin bash wrapper that `exec`s `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/hermes`. **Any config/auth change affects both GUI and CLI.** There is no separate per-surface config.
- **Source install:** `.install_method=git`, source in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/`. Official updates go through `hermes update`; backups land in `~/.hermes/backups/`.
- **Full docs are local:** `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/` holds the complete Docusaurus doc site (339 `.md` files). Prefer it over the web. `hermes update` replaces the source dir but keeps this relative path stable.
- A local copy of Hermes's own bundled ops skill lives at `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` — useful as a fuller (but list-heavy, faster-staling) reference. Its lists are exactly what this skill routes around; treat its **stable** guidance (safety, gotchas) as aligned with the rules below.

## Routing — intent → reference file + discovery command

| You want to… | Read | Discover current truth with |
|---|---|---|
| Configure / switch provider or model, edit any config | `reference/config-editing.md` | `~/.hermes/config.yaml` or `hermes config show`; `hermes model`; `hermes config --help`, `hermes auth --help` |
| Run / use it day-to-day (chat, TUI, sessions, cron, send) | `reference/operations.md` | `hermes --help`; `hermes <cmd> --help`; `/help` in-session |
| Diagnose / maintain / update / roll back | `reference/troubleshooting.md` | `hermes doctor`, `hermes status`, `hermes logs` |

**Discovery commands (what exists right now, never copied into this skill):**

- Subcommands: `hermes --help`, then `hermes <cmd> --help` for flags.
- Config: read `~/.hermes/config.yaml` directly, or `hermes config show`; paths via `hermes config path` / `hermes config env-path`.
- Tools: `hermes tools list`. Skills: `hermes skills list`. Providers/models: `hermes model`.
- In-session slash commands: `/help`.
- Anything deeper or uncovered: **Rule 1** — grep the local docs at `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/` (full offline doc set), web index only as fallback.

## Safety norms (aligned with Hermes's bundled autonomous-ai-agents skill)

These are adopted directly from Hermes's own ops skill — don't invent a parallel set. Details and the exact edit commands are in `reference/config-editing.md`.

- **Edit config with the dedicated commands, not blind YAML edits:** `hermes config edit` / `hermes config set KEY VAL`. Settings live in `config.yaml`; secrets live in `.env`.
- **API keys go through `hermes auth`** (the credential manager), not hand-edited `.env`. Never print secrets in plaintext, never commit them to any repo.
- **`security.redact_secrets` is on by default** and snapshotted at process startup — toggling it mid-session does NOT take effect; it needs a restart. This is deliberate, so an LLM can't disable redaction on itself mid-task.
- **Command approval (`approvals.mode`):** `manual` (default — prompts before destructive commands) / `smart` / `off` (equivalent to `--yolo`). YOLO / `off` does NOT disable secret redaction; they're independent.
- **Most config, tool, and skill changes need a restart to take effect:** exit and relaunch the CLI; `/restart` the gateway; `/reset` (new session) for tool/skill changes. Confirm before running anything destructive in `manual`-mode environments.

These are adopted directly from Hermes's own ops skill — don't invent a parallel set. Details and the exact edit commands are in `reference/config-editing.md`.

- **Edit config with the dedicated commands, not blind YAML edits:** `hermes config edit` / `hermes config set KEY VAL`. Settings live in `config.yaml`; secrets live in `.env`.
- **API keys go through `hermes auth`** (the credential manager), not hand-edited `.env`. Never print secrets in plaintext, never commit them to any repo.
- **`security.redact_secrets` is on by default** and snapshotted at process startup — toggling it mid-session does NOT take effect; it needs a restart. This is deliberate, so an LLM can't disable redaction on itself mid-task.
- **Command approval (`approvals.mode`):** `manual` (default — prompts before destructive commands) / `smart` / `off` (equivalent to `--yolo`). YOLO / `off` does NOT disable secret redaction; they're independent.
- **Most config, tool, and skill changes need a restart to take effect:** exit and relaunch the CLI; `/restart` the gateway; `/reset` (new session) for tool/skill changes. Confirm before running anything destructive in `manual`-mode environments.

# Config Editing & Provider/Model Workflow

Stable mechanics for changing Hermes's configuration safely, plus the ordered workflow for switching providers/models so the change actually takes effect. Specific keys, sections, and provider names are **not** listed here — they go stale. Discover them live (see pointers below).

> Before running any command, confirm its flags with `hermes <cmd> --help` (SKILL.md Rule 2). If something here doesn't cover your case, fetch the docs index (Rule 1).

## Safe config-editing norms (adopted from Hermes's bundled autonomous-ai-agents skill)

Source (local): `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` and the docs page `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/user-guide/skills/bundled/autonomous-ai-agents/` (web mirror: `https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/skills/bundled/autonomous-ai-agents/`).

- **Use the dedicated commands, not blind YAML editing.** `hermes config edit` opens the file in `$EDITOR`; `hermes config set KEY VAL` sets a single value (dotted keys, e.g. `section.key`). These keep the file valid and avoid clobbering structure.
- **Settings vs secrets are separate stores.** Non-secret settings live in `config.yaml`; API keys and tokens live in `.env`. Find the paths with `hermes config path` and `hermes config env-path`.
- **API keys go through `hermes auth`** (the credential manager / pool), not hand-edited `.env`. Don't print keys in plaintext and never commit them to any repo.
- **Validate after editing:** `hermes config check` (missing/outdated config) and `hermes config migrate` (pull in new options / migrate deprecated settings). For models that have been retired, the top-level `hermes migrate` handles config migration too — check `hermes migrate --help` vs `hermes config migrate --help` for which applies.
- **`security.redact_secrets` is on by default** and snapshotted at startup. Toggling it mid-session (even via an env var from a tool call) does NOT affect the running process — change it from a terminal, then start a new session. This is intentional, so the agent can't turn off redaction on itself.
- **`approvals.mode`:** `manual` (default; prompts before destructive shell commands) / `smart` (auxiliary LLM auto-approves low-risk) / `off` (skips all prompts, same as `--yolo`). Per-invocation bypass: `hermes --yolo …`. YOLO/`off` does NOT disable secret redaction.
- **Restart to apply.** Config changes need a fresh process: exit and relaunch the CLI, or `/restart` the gateway. Tool/skill changes apply on `/reset` (new session), not mid-conversation (mid-conversation changes would break prompt caching).

> **The list of config sections, keys, defaults, and current values is not copied here** — it's the most stale-prone thing. To see what exists and what's set: read `~/.hermes/config.yaml` directly, or run `hermes config show`. For the full annotated reference, read the local doc `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/user-guide/configuration.md` (web mirror: `https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/configuration`).

## Provider / model workflow (ordered — this is the stable part)

The point of ordering: a model/provider switch only "works" if the credential exists first and the process is restarted after. Do these in order.

1. **Configure the provider's credential first.** `hermes auth` (interactive) or `hermes auth add <provider>`. If you select a provider in the model picker before its credential is set up, it'll only fail at runtime. Verify with `hermes auth list` / `hermes auth status`.
2. **Select the default model / provider.** `hermes model` (interactive picker; `--refresh` re-fetches each provider's live model list). The persistent provider lives in `config.yaml` under `model.provider`.
3. **Configure fallback** (tried when the primary model fails — rate-limit, overload, connection errors). `hermes fallback --help` for `add` / `list` / `remove` / `clear`.
4. **Restart so it takes effect.** Exit and relaunch the CLI, or `/restart` the gateway.
5. **Verify what's actually live.** `hermes status` (add `--all` for a redacted full view) and `hermes model` confirm which provider/model is in effect — don't assume the edit took.

> **The provider list and their env-var / auth methods are not copied here** (they change often). To see what's available and how each authenticates: run `hermes model`, or read the local doc `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/integrations/providers.md` (web mirror: `https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/integrations/providers`). For a one-off override without changing config: `hermes -m <model> --provider <provider> …` (see `hermes chat --help`).

To see what's available and how each authenticates: run `hermes model`, or read the local doc `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/integrations/providers.md` (web mirror: `https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/integrations/providers`). For a one-off override without changing config: `hermes -m <model> --provider <provider> …` (see `hermes chat --help`).

lines: - **Validate after editing:** `hermes config check` (missing/outdated config) and `hermes config migrate` (pull in new options / migrate deprecated settings). Retiring specific deprecated models is a separate, narrower command — `hermes migrate` (currently `hermes migrate xai`); run `hermes migrate --help` to see what it currently covers.

- **`security.redact_secrets` is on by default** and snapshotted at startup. Toggling it mid-session (even via an env var from a tool call) does NOT affect the running process — change it from a terminal, then start a new session. This is intentional, so the agent can't turn off redaction on itself.

- **`approvals.mode`:** `manual` (default; prompts before destructive shell commands) / `smart` (auxiliary LLM auto-approves low-risk) / `off` (skips all prompts, same as `--yolo`). Per-invocation bypass: `hermes --yolo …`. YOLO/`off` does NOT disable secret redaction.

-- **Restart to apply.** Config changes need a fresh process: exit and relaunch the CLI, or `/restart` the gateway. Tool/skill changes apply on `/reset` (new session), not mid-conversation (mid-conversation changes would break prompt caching).

+- **Restart to apply.** A config edit only takes effect in a fresh process: exit and relaunch the CLI, or `/restart` the gateway. (Full restart mapping for tool/skill/source changes: `reference/troubleshooting.md`.)

> **The list of config sections, keys, defaults, and current values is not copied here** — it's the most stale-prone thing. To see what exists and what's set: read `~/.hermes/config.yaml` directly, or run `hermes config show`. For the full annotated reference, read the local doc `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/user-guide/configuration.md` (web mirror: `https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/configuration`).

# Troubleshooting & Recovery

Stable diagnostic and rollback **processes**. Symptom lists go stale and are not copied here — when a symptom isn't covered, fetch the docs index (SKILL.md Rule 1). Confirm flags with `hermes <cmd> --help` before running (Rule 2).

## Diagnostic triad (start here, in order)

1. `hermes doctor` — checks config and dependencies. `hermes doctor --fix` attempts automatic fixes. It also surfaces security advisories (ack with `hermes doctor --ack <id>`).
2. `hermes status` — component status; `hermes status --all` for a redacted full view, `--deep` for slower thorough checks.
3. Logs — `hermes logs` (defaults to `agent.log`, last 50 lines). Useful flags: `-f` (follow), `--level ERROR`, `--component gateway|agent|cron`, `--since 1h`, `hermes logs list` to see available files. Files live under `~/.hermes/logs/`.

## Common failure shapes (process, not symptom catalog)

- **Model / provider not working:** `hermes doctor` first → re-authenticate the provider with `hermes auth` (or `hermes auth add <provider>`) → confirm the key/credential exists. See `reference/config-editing.md` for the full provider workflow.
- **A change didn't take effect:** this is almost always a restart issue, not a broken edit.
  - Tool / skill changes → `/reset` (new session); they don't apply mid-conversation.
  - Config changes → `/restart` the gateway, or exit and relaunch the CLI.
  - Source-code changes → restart the process entirely.
- **Update broke the install / rollback:** Hermes keeps backups in `~/.hermes/backups/`; restore one with `hermes import <zipfile>` (`--force` to overwrite). A `~/.hermes/hermes-agent.broken-<timestamp>` directory is the old source set aside by a failed/risky `hermes update` — it's a leftover, not active. You can also create your own safety net before risky operations with `hermes backup` (`--quick` for just critical state). Filesystem checkpoints (the `/rollback` history) are managed via `hermes checkpoints` (`status` / `prune` / `clear`).
- **Retired / deprecated model or settings after an update:** `hermes config migrate` (and/or top-level `hermes migrate` for retired models — check each `--help`).
- **Anything else / deeper:** consult the local docs (Rule 1) — start with `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/reference/faq.md`, or `grep -ril <symptom> ~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/`. The local bundled skill at `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` has a longer (faster-staling) troubleshooting section worth skimming for gateway/platform-specific cases.

## Recovery sequencing (when an update or edit left it broken)

1. **Diagnose before changing anything** — `hermes doctor` and `hermes status` to see what's actually wrong; check `hermes logs --level ERROR`.
2. **Snapshot current state** — `hermes backup --quick` before you start undoing things, so a bad recovery is itself reversible.
3. **Restore the most recent good backup** — `hermes import <zipfile>` from `~/.hermes/backups/`.
4. **Re-validate** — `hermes config check`, then `hermes doctor` again; relaunch and confirm with `hermes status`.

# Day-to-Day Operations

Stable run **patterns**. Exact flags and the full subcommand list go stale — confirm with `hermes <cmd> --help` before running (SKILL.md Rule 2), and discover what exists with `hermes --help`. For anything uncovered, fetch the docs index (Rule 1).

## Running it

- **Interactive (default):** `hermes` drops straight into chat. Force a surface with `hermes chat --tui` or `hermes chat --cli`.
- **One-shot / scriptable:** `hermes chat -q "…"` runs a single query non-interactively. The top-level `hermes -z "…"` (`--oneshot`) prints only the final response text (no banner/spinner/tools preview) — intended for pipes and scripts; approvals are auto-bypassed in this mode, so treat it accordingly.
- **Continue a conversation:** `--continue` (most recent, or by name) / `--resume <session-id>`. These are flags, not subcommands — `hermes chat --help` for the exact forms.
- **GUI / desktop app:** `hermes desktop` (alias `hermes gui`) builds and launches the Electron app. It shares the same `~/.hermes/` home as the CLI.

## Sessions

`hermes sessions` manages the session store (list, browse, rename, export, prune, delete, stats). Subcommands and their flags shift — run `hermes sessions --help`. In-session, `/resume`, `/branch`, and history commands are available via `/help`.

## Automation

- **Scheduled jobs:** `hermes cron` (create/list/edit/pause/resume/run/remove). Schedules accept durations (`30m`), "every" phrases, 5-field cron, or ISO timestamps. `hermes cron --help`, then the specific subcommand's `--help`. Prefer cron over spawning processes for recurring work — it handles delivery and retry.
- **Send a message to a configured platform** (from scripts/cron/CI, no agent loop): `hermes send` — reuses the gateway's platform credentials. `hermes send --list` shows available targets; `hermes send --help` for target syntax (`platform`, `platform:chat_id`, `platform:#channel`).

## In-session slash commands

Type `/help` inside a chat session for the current, authoritative list — it is not copied here because new commands land often. Common stable ones: `/reset` (new session), `/restart` (gateway), `/model`, `/config`, `/rollback`, `/skill <name>`.

## Deeper areas (one-line pointers — not expanded here)

These exist and are powerful, but are out of this skill's day-to-day scope. When you actually need one, fetch its guide from the docs index (Rule 1):

- **Multi-instance / tmux orchestration** and **`delegate_task`** for parallel or long-running agents.
- **delegation**, **curator** (background skill maintenance), and **kanban** (multi-agent work queue) subsystems.
- **MCP servers**, **plugins**, **profiles**, **webhooks**, and **gateway** platform setup.

The local bundled skill at `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` has worked examples for spawning and tmux orchestration if you need a starting point.

+Type `/help` inside a chat session for the current, authoritative list — it is not copied here because new commands land often. (A handful recur in this skill's workflows: `/reset` for a new session, `/restart` for the gateway.)

content.text:
Review 已完成并据此修复。校验通过(exit 0)。

## TASK 1 — 路径/命令核验

Subagent 逐项实测,**95% 准确**,文件路径全部存在,所有 `hermes` 子命令均真实存在。发现一个真 bug + 一处措辞过头,我已自行复核后修复:

- **Finding A(真 bug)**:`~/.hermes/backups/` **不存在**。我之前把它当作 `hermes update` 的固定备份目录写死了 3 处——实际上 `updates.pre_update_backup: false`(从未生成),而 `hermes backup` 默认输出是 `~/hermes-backup-<timestamp>.zip`。已改为不硬编码路径,改用 `ls ~/hermes-backup-*.zip` + `hermes backup/update --help` 发现(符合技能自身的「路由,不写死」原则)。
- **Finding B(措辞)**:`hermes migrate` 目前只有 `xai` 子命令,我把它说成通用退役模型迁移器略夸大。已收敛为「针对特定退役模型的窄命令,以 `hermes migrate --help` 为准」。
- 顺带确认 `.install_method=git` 是文件内容(准确),已标注它是文件而非 config 键。

## TASK 2 — 精简

按「影响优先」处理了 review 列出的重复/填充:

1. **安全规范双写**(SKILL.md ↔ config-editing.md):SKILL.md 收为 4 条「前门要点」,完整编辑规范交回 config-editing.md 独家持有。
2. **重启语义三处重复**:以 troubleshooting.md 为唯一权威,SKILL.md 与 config-editing.md 改为交叉引用,消除措辞漂移。
3. **bundled-skill 指针四处**:保留 troubleshooting/operations 的「指向具体内容」的两处,SKILL.md 留一处权威说明,config-editing.md 去掉重复解释。
4. **填充句**:删掉 SKILL.md 顶部 meta 叙述段,压成一句。
5. **operations.md 的 slash 命令清单**:从 6 个收为「仅 `/help` 权威 + 2 个工作流里复现的」,贴合「不存副本」。

所有改动通过 `npx skills add --list` 只读校验。未自动提交。

---
name: hermes-agent
description: "Use when configuring, running, or maintaining a Hermes Agent (Nous Research's open-source agent framework) that is ALREADY installed on this machine — switching its model or provider, editing any config, running it day-to-day (chat/TUI/sessions/cron/send), or troubleshooting and repairing it (updates, rollback, doctor, logs, backup restore). Trigger even when the user just says 'Hermes is broken', 'change Hermes's model', 'how do I run Hermes in the background', or names `~/.hermes/`, `~/.local/bin/hermes`, or `/Applications/Hermes.app`. Does NOT cover first-time install/bootstrap, contributing to Hermes's source, or Windows-only issues."
---

# Hermes Agent — Operations

Hermes Agent (Nous Research) is already installed on this machine. This skill helps you **configure, run, and troubleshoot** it. It does not cover first-time install, source-code contribution, or Windows.

Hermes changes fast, so stale-prone lists (subcommands, providers, config sections, slash commands, symptoms) are **not** copied here — you discover those live at runtime. This skill keeps only the stable stuff: the two hard rules, machine facts, workflows, safety norms, and gotchas.

## Two hard rules (these outrank answering from memory)

**Rule 1 — When unsure or uncovered, consult the docs. Do not guess.**
This machine has the **complete doc set locally** (git install) — prefer it over the web; it's the same content, offline and greppable. The docs root is:

```
~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/        # 339 .md files, full Docusaurus site
```

Grep/glob there to find the relevant guide (e.g. `grep -ril <topic> ~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/`), then read the file. Key sections: `user-guide/`, `integrations/`, `reference/`, `developer-guide/`. If the local docs are absent (non-git install) or you still can't find it, fall back to the web index and route from it to the specific guide URL:

```
https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/assets/files/llms-d4972c57170916efd83766ae50c3bb3d.txt
```

Never invent commands, flags, config keys, or provider names.

**Rule 2 — Verify exact command flags/arguments with `--help` before running.**
Hermes's subcommands and flags shift between versions. Before you run any specific command, check it live:

```bash
hermes <subcommand> --help        # e.g. hermes config --help, hermes auth --help
```

Do not reconstruct flags from memory. The `--help` output is the source of truth for what exists right now.

## Machine facts (stable structure — use for orientation)

- **Home:** `~/.hermes/` — config (`config.yaml`), secrets (`.env`), `auth.json`, `logs/`, `sessions/`, `state.db`, `skills/`, source at `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/`.
- **GUI and CLI share the same home.** `/Applications/Hermes.app` and the CLI `~/.local/bin/hermes` both run against `~/.hermes/`. The CLI is a thin bash wrapper that `exec`s `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/hermes`. **Any config/auth change affects both GUI and CLI.** There is no separate per-surface config.
- **Source install:** `.install_method=git` (the file `~/.hermes/.install_method`), source in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/`. Official updates go through `hermes update`, which can take a pre-update backup (see `hermes update --help`; gated by `updates.pre_update_backup`, off by default here). Don't assume a fixed backup path — locate backups per `reference/troubleshooting.md`.
- **Full docs are local:** `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/` holds the complete Docusaurus doc site (339 `.md` files). Prefer it over the web. `hermes update` replaces the source dir but keeps this relative path stable.
- A local copy of Hermes's own bundled ops skill lives at `~/.hermes/skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md` — useful as a fuller (but list-heavy, faster-staling) reference. Its lists are exactly what this skill routes around; treat its **stable** guidance (safety, gotchas) as aligned with the rules below.

## Routing — intent → reference file + discovery command

| You want to… | Read | Discover current truth with |
|---|---|---|
| Configure / switch provider or model, edit any config | `reference/config-editing.md` | `~/.hermes/config.yaml` or `hermes config show`; `hermes model`; `hermes config --help`, `hermes auth --help` |
| Run / use it day-to-day (chat, TUI, sessions, cron, send) | `reference/operations.md` | `hermes --help`; `hermes <cmd> --help`; `/help` in-session |
| Diagnose / maintain / update / roll back | `reference/troubleshooting.md` | `hermes doctor`, `hermes status`, `hermes logs` |

**Discovery commands (what exists right now, never copied into this skill):**

- Subcommands: `hermes --help`, then `hermes <cmd> --help` for flags.
- Config: read `~/.hermes/config.yaml` directly, or `hermes config show`; paths via `hermes config path` / `hermes config env-path`.
- Tools: `hermes tools list`. Skills: `hermes skills list`. Providers/models: `hermes model`.
- In-session slash commands: `/help`.
- Anything deeper or uncovered: **Rule 1** — grep the local docs at `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/website/docs/` (full offline doc set), web index only as fallback.

## Safety norms (aligned with Hermes's bundled autonomous-ai-agents skill)

These are adopted directly from Hermes's own ops skill — don't invent a parallel set. The full editing norms (dedicated `config`/`auth` commands, config-vs-`.env` split, validation) live in `reference/config-editing.md`; the front-door essentials:

- **Secrets:** API keys go through `hermes auth`, not hand-edited `.env`. Never print secrets in plaintext or commit them to any repo.
- **`security.redact_secrets` is on by default** and snapshotted at process startup — toggling it mid-session does NOT take effect (needs a restart). Deliberate, so an LLM can't disable redaction on itself mid-task.
- **Command approval (`approvals.mode`):** `manual` (default — prompts before destructive commands) / `smart` / `off` (equivalent to `--yolo`). YOLO / `off` does NOT disable secret redaction; they're independent. Confirm before destructive actions in `manual` environments.
- **Changes need a restart to apply** — full mapping (tool/skill → `/reset`, config → `/restart` or relaunch) in `reference/troubleshooting.md`.
